channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Challenges in Tanzania’s Dairy Sector Limit Local Milk Supply Despite Large Herds
item.description: Tanzania get big number ya livestock, lakini bado inabidi ile milk nyingi ije kutoka nje. Experts wanasema si kwamba milk haipatikani, bali kuna problem ya quality, infrastructure na market organisati...

item.title: Delhi Court Sentences Kashmiri Separatist Leader Asiya Andrabi to Life Imprisonment
item.description: On Tuesday, a Delhi court handed down a life imprisonment sentence to Kashmiri separatist leader Asiya Andrabi, who is alleged to be the founder and chief of the banned all-women separatist group Dukh...

item.title: Cebu Pacific adjusts flights amid rising global fuel costs
item.description: Cebu Pacific, the Philippines’ leading airline, has announced cancellations and reductions of several overseas flights in response to a sharp increase in global fuel prices, which have more than doubl...

item.title: Tezgam Express derails near Lodhran injuring 25 passengers, eight critical, track blocked
item.description: A late-night derailment of the Tezgam Express train occurred near Adam Wahan close to Lodhran on Tuesday night. The Karachi-bound train from Peshawar had at least four coaches jump the tracks, with so...

item.title: 12 Senior SAPS Officers Arrested for Alleged Involvement in Corruption Linked to ‘Cat’ Matlala’s Company
item.description: In a significant development, twelve senior South African Police Service (SAPS) officers have been arrested for their alleged involvement in an irregular tender that was awarded to the company Medicar...

item.title: Mohamed Salah set to depart Liverpool after nine successful seasons
item.description: Liverpool have confirmed that Mohamed Salah will leave the club at the end of the 2025-26 season, bringing an end to an illustrious nine-year stint at Anfield. The 33-year-old Egyptian forward joined ...

item.title: Nigel Biggar Critiques Woke Culture in Academia
item.description: In his latest book, 'The New Dark Age: Why Liberals Must Win the Culture Wars,' Oxford professor Nigel Biggar argues that modern progressive movements, particularly in universities, threaten open soci...

item.title: Day 25 of US-Israel War on Iran: Tactical Pause but Structural Expansion Continues
item.description: On the 25th day of the ongoing US-Israeli war against Iran, the situation shows a delicate balance where a five-day pause announced by President Donald Trump on strikes targeting Iranian energy infras...

item.title: Nakuru Water Officer Arrested for Allegedly Demanding Bribe to Replace Water Meter
item.description: An officer working for Nakuru Water and Sewerage Company (NAWASCO) was arrested by the Ethics and Anti-Corruption Commission (EACC) following accusations of soliciting a bribe. Leonard Cheruiyot Mutai...

item.title: Henry Holland Opens Up About His Shopping Habits and Fashion Choices
item.description: Fashion designer Henry Holland, known for his iconic ‘fashion groupie’ T-shirts, gives an insight into his shopping habits, from quirky gifts for loved ones to splurging on personal trainers. Holland,...

item.title: Lok Sabha Passes Amendment to Transgender Rights Bill, Faces Criticism from Opposition
item.description: The Lok Sabha passed the Transgender Persons Protection of Rights Amendment Bill on Tuesday, which aims to amend the 2019 law concerning transgender rights. The bill removes the right for transgender ...

item.title: Justice Ayo Salami Questions Peter Obi's Eligibility in 2023 Presidential Race Over Party Membership Issues
item.description: Retired Justice Ayo Salami, former President of the Court of Appeal, don talk say former Anambra Governor Peter Obi no suppose to don contest the 2023 presidential election under Labour Party (LP). He...

item.title: Four Canadian premiers call for greater provincial role in federal judicial appointments
item.description: Four Canadian premiers—Alberta’s Danielle Smith, Saskatchewan’s Scott Moe, Ontario’s Doug Ford, and Quebec’s François Legault—have sent a joint letter to Prime Minister Mark Carney urging a more colla...

item.title: UN Expert Accuses Israel of Torture as State Policy in Occupied Palestinian Territories
item.description: Francesca Albanese, the UN's special rapporteur for the Palestinian territories, has condemned Israel for what she claims is the adoption of torture as state policy. Albanese presented her report at t...

item.title: The Swasthani Vrata Katha: An Ancient Nepali Hindu Text and Its Complex Impact on Women's Lives
item.description: The Swasthani Vrata Katha, a 16th-century Nepali Hindu devotional text, remains a vibrant part of winter rituals in Nepal. Each year during the cold lunar month of January-February, many Hindu women i...

item.title: Edmonton International Airport announces daily Porter Airlines flights to Montreal beginning June 16
item.description: Edmonton International Airport (YEG) has announced the addition of seven weekly flights to Montreal, operated by Porter Airlines, set to begin on June 16, 2026. This new service will provide daily non...

item.title: Salah set to leave Liverpool after long nine-year spell
item.description: Mohamed Salah has decided to leave Liverpool at the end of the current season. The club confirmed that the Egyptian forward will part ways with the Reds after an impressive nine-year spell. Salah, who...

item.title: Sonia Gandhi admitted to Sir Ganga Ram Hospital in Delhi for observation and treatment
item.description: Senior Congress leader and Rajya Sabha MP Sonia Gandhi was admitted to Sir Ganga Ram Hospital in New Delhi late on Tuesday evening. According to hospital sources, she is currently undergoing treatment...

item.title: Challenges and Effects of Long Distances Students Walk to School in Tanzania
item.description: In Tanzania, many students face serious challenges because dey must walk long distances to get to school every day. Dis problem is especially common in rural areas where schools are far from villages,...

item.title: Unesco commits to strengthen Tanzania higher education with focus on gender equality in ICT
item.description: The United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (Unesco) has promised to keep working with the Tanzanian government to improve higher education systems across the country. The mai...
